corpus.note: Coherent set: captions.json, clip_embeddings.pkl and text_embeddings.pkl share an identical filename list with 0 stubs. One recurring subject, ~25 years.

shared_artifacts.captions.json: 227 structured-JSON captions (schema: one_line, subject_age_band, subject_appearance, subject_activity, companions, setting, occasion, objects, searchable_text)

l2_artifacts.l2_route_comparison.json: 6-query CLIP-vs-caption top-3 results (visual vs semantic route)

l3_artifacts.l3/evaluation_results.json: precision@{1,3,5} for visual_only / caption_only / hybrid, plus per-query
l3_artifacts.l3/gold_queries.json: 8 queries with relevant filenames (auto-derived from caption fields + filename dates; NOT hand-verified)

honesty_notes: Gold set is auto-derived from caption fields and eyeballed, not exhaustively hand-verified; caption/hybrid scores are biased upward relative to visual because labels share the caption text.
8 queries and precision@{1,3,5}: directional, not tight confidence intervals.
A single global alpha is optimised; per-query weighting is left as future work.
The 1462-run backup (628/1462 usable) lives in the L2 history as evidence of scale brittleness; it is not required for any figure here.
notes: captions.json / clip_embeddings.pkl / text_embeddings.pkl are byte-shared across L2 and L3 (same corpus). All figures are regenerated from these artifacts by figures/make_figures.py.
